text:
When setting project goals, it's important to use the SMART framework. SMART stands for Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," set a goal like "increase website traffic by 20% within three months."

Break the project into smaller milestones, such as completing the design phase by week two. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, like a design prototype or test results. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment. This means checking with everyone involved that they agree on the goals and timeline. When all team members and clients share the same understanding, the project runs more smoothly and avoids confusion later.
